trade.summary: Corea del Sud is one of Israele's top Asian trading partners. Israele exports diamonds, pharmaceuticals, and electronic components; Corea del Sud exports semiconductors, electronics, and vehicles. A free trade agreement has been in force since 2021, boosting bilateral commerce.

diplomatic.summary: Israele and Corea del Sud share democratic values and have strong diplomatic ties since 1962. The FTA signed in 2021 elevated the relationship. Corea del Sud has expressed solidarity with Israele but maintains careful neutrality on the Gaza conflict due to Arab market interests and UN dynamics.

military.summary: Significant defense cooperation. Israele has sold radar systems, UAVs, and other defense technology to Corea del Sud. Both face regional adversaries and share intelligence on missile programs. Corea del Sud has modeled aspects of its Iron Dome-equivalent programs on Israelei systems.

financial.summary: Growing bilateral investment, particularly in technology sectors. Corea del Sudn conglomerates have invested in Israelei startups; Israelei firms seek Corea del Sudn manufacturing partnerships. The FTA has expanded financial linkages.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Corea del Sud imports minimal Israelei energy products. Some cooperation on energy technology. Corea del Sud is interested in Eastern Mediterranean LNG as a future option.

scientific.summary: Active S&T cooperation through bilateral agreement. Joint research in AI, biotech, and semiconductors. Israelei and Corea del Sudn researchers collaborate across top universities. Growing trend of joint startup ventures.
